classification_rules.borderline: Position language present (temporal/sequence/per-position narration) but the gold answer is a content string that is order-invariant; documented individually for human review.
classification_rules.exclude: Question/options are position-referential such that reordering images changes the true gold: options enumerate image positions ('first image','image 2','(A) Image 1'); question asks to IDENTIFY/SELECT an image ('which image shows/best depicts ...') with image-index options; options map each image POSITION to a label; answer is a SEQUENCE of image positions; or absolute spatial/order language is load-bearing.
classification_rules.keep: Answer text is order-invariant: counting across all images, set-membership ('not depicted in any image'), attribute questions with TEXTUAL answer options, or a diagnosis/finding derived from the combined frame set.

public_sanitization: Question text, choices, and free-text rationales are omitted here to avoid redistributing upstream dataset content. Use item IDs with the upstream datasets to inspect the original records under their licenses.
purpose: Identify image_set_order items whose fixed (position-letter) gold is invalidated when the image sequence is permuted, i.e. position-referential items where reordering the images changes the TRUE gold. Such items must be excluded from order-robustness scoring because the as-scored gold is tied to an image slot, not to content.
title: Image-set position-reference screen (image_set_order facet)
